column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en

BG 3.1#अर्जुन उवाच
ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन
तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे
तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम्#arjuna uvācha
jyāyasī chet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdana
tat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśhava
vyāmiśhreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva me
tad ekaṁ vada niśhchitya yena śhreyo ’ham āpnuyām#BG 3.1-2:#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said
column8: janārdana—he who looks after the public, Krishna
column15: keśhava—Krishna, the killer of the demon named Keshi

column1:
BG 3.2#अर्जुन उवाच
ज्यायसी चेत्कर्मणस्ते मता बुद्धिर्जनार्दन
तत्किं कर्मणि घोरे मां नियोजयसि केशव
व्यामिश्रेणेव वाक्येन बुद्धिं मोहयसीव मे
तदेकं वद निश्चित्य येन श्रेयोऽहमाप्नुयाम्#arjuna uvācha
jyāyasī chet karmaṇas te matā buddhir janārdana
tat kiṁ karmaṇi ghore māṁ niyojayasi keśhava
vyāmiśhreṇeva vākyena buddhiṁ mohayasīva me
tad ekaṁ vada niśhchitya yena śhreyo ’ham āpnuyām#BG 3.1-2:#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said
column8: janārdana—he who looks after the public, Krishna
column15: keśhava—Krishna, the killer of the demon named Keshi

column1:
BG 3.3#श्रीभगवानुवाच
लोकेऽस्मिन्द्विविधा निष्ठा पुरा प्रोक्ता मयानघ
ज्ञानयोगेन साङ्ख्यानां कर्मयोगेन योगिनाम्#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
loke ’smin dvi-vidhā niṣhṭhā purā proktā mayānagha
jñāna-yogena sāṅkhyānāṁ karma-yogena yoginām#The Lord said: O sinless one, the two paths leading to enlightenment were previously explained by Me: the path of knowledge, for those inclined toward contemplation, and the path of work for those inclined toward action.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Lord said

BG 3.4#न कर्मणामनारम्भान्नैष्कर्म्यं पुरुषोऽश्नुते\nन च संन्यसनादेव सिद्धिं समधिगच्छति#na karmaṇām anārambhān naiṣhkarmyaṁ puruṣho ’śhnute\nna cha sannyasanād eva siddhiṁ samadhigachchhati#One cannot achieve freedom from karmic reactions by merely abstaining from work, nor can one attain perfection of knowledge by mere physical renunciation.#na—not

BG 3.5#न हि कश्चित्क्षणमपि जातु तिष्ठत्यकर्मकृत्\nकार्यते ह्यवश: कर्म सर्व: प्रकृतिजैर्गुणै:#na hi kaśhchit kṣhaṇam api jātu tiṣhṭhatyakarma-kṛit\nkāryate hyavaśhaḥ karma sarvaḥ prakṛiti-jair guṇaiḥ#There is no one who can remain without action even for a moment. Indeed, all beings are compelled to act by their qualities born of material nature (the three guṇas).#na—not

BG 3.6#कर्मेन्द्रियाणि संयम्य य आस्ते मनसा स्मरन्\nइन्द्रियार्थान्विमूढात्मा मिथ्याचार: स उच्यते#karmendriyāṇi sanyamya ya āste manasā smaran\nindriyārthān vimūḍhātmā mithyāchāraḥ sa uchyate#Those who restrain the external organs of action, while continuing to dwell on sense objects in the mind, certainly delude themselves and are to be called hypocrites.#karma-indriyāṇi—organs of action

BG 3.7#यस्त्विन्द्रियाणि मनसा नियम्यारभतेऽर्जुन\nकर्मेन्द्रियै: कर्मयोगमसक्त: स विशिष्यते#yas tvindriyāṇi manasā niyamyārabhate ’rjuna\nkarmendriyaiḥ karma-yogam asaktaḥ sa viśhiṣhyate#But those karm yogis who control their knowledge senses with the mind, O Arjun, and engage the working senses in working without attachment, are certainly superior.#yaḥ—who

BG 3.8#नियतं कुरु कर्म त्वं कर्म ज्यायो ह्यकर्मण:\nशरीरयात्रापि च ते न प्रसिद्ध्येदकर्मण:#niyataṁ kuru karma tvaṁ karma jyāyo hyakarmaṇaḥ\nśharīra-yātrāpi cha te na prasiddhyed akarmaṇaḥ#You should thus perform your prescribed Vedic duties, since action is superior to inaction. By ceasing activity, even your bodily maintenance will not be possible.#niyatam—constantly

BG 3.9#यज्ञार्थात्कर्मणोऽन्यत्र लोकोऽयं कर्मबन्धन:\nतदर्थं कर्म कौन्तेय मुक्तसङ्ग: समाचर#yajñārthāt karmaṇo ’nyatra loko ’yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ\ntad-arthaṁ karma kaunteya mukta-saṅgaḥ samāchara#Work must be done as a yajna to the Supreme Lord
column2: otherwise, work causes bondage in this material world. Therefore, O son of Kunti, for the satisfaction of God, perform your prescribed duties, without being attached to the results.#yajña-arthāt—for the sake of sacrifice

column1: BG 3.10#सहयज्ञा: प्रजा: सृष्ट्वा पुरोवाच प्रजापति:\nअनेन प्रसविष्यध्वमेष वोऽस्त्विष्टकामधुक्#saha-yajñāḥ prajāḥ sṛiṣhṭvā purovācha prajāpatiḥ\nanena prasaviṣhyadhvam eṣha vo ’stviṣhṭa-kāma-dhuk#In the beginning of creation, Brahma created humankind along with duties, and said, “Prosper in the performance of these yajñas (sacrifices), for they shall bestow upon you all you wish to achieve.”#saha—along with

BG 3.11#देवान्भावयतानेन ते देवा भावयन्तु व:\nपरस्परं भावयन्त: श्रेय: परमवाप्स्यथ#devān bhāvayatānena te devā bhāvayantu vaḥ\nparasparaṁ bhāvayantaḥ śhreyaḥ param avāpsyatha#By your sacrifices, the celestial gods will be pleased, and by cooperation between humans and the celestial gods, great prosperity will reign for all.#devān—celestial gods

BG 3.12#इष्टान्भोगान्हि वो देवा दास्यन्ते यज्ञभाविता:\nतैर्दत्तानप्रदायैभ्यो यो भुङ्क्ते स्तेन एव स:#iṣhṭān bhogān hi vo devā dāsyante yajña-bhāvitāḥ\ntair dattān apradāyaibhyo yo bhuṅkte stena eva saḥ#The celestial gods, being satisfied by the performance of sacrifice, will grant you all the desired necessities of life. But those who enjoy what is given to them, without making offerings in return, are verily thieves.#iṣhṭān—desired

BG 3.13#यज्ञशिष्टाशिन: सन्तो मुच्यन्ते सर्वकिल्बिषै:\nभुञ्जते ते त्वघं पापा ये पचन्त्यात्मकारणात्#yajña-śhiṣhṭāśhinaḥ santo muchyante sarva-kilbiṣhaiḥ\nbhuñjate te tvaghaṁ pāpā ye pachantyātma-kāraṇāt#The spiritually-minded, who eat food that is first offered in sacrifice, are released from all kinds of sin. Others, who cook food for their own enjoyment, verily eat only sin.#yajña-śhiṣhṭa—remnants of food offered in sacrifice

BG 3.14#अन्नाद्भवन्ति भूतानि पर्जन्यादन्नसम्भव:\nयज्ञाद्भवति पर्जन्यो यज्ञ: कर्मसमुद्भव:#annād bhavanti bhūtāni parjanyād anna-sambhavaḥ\nyajñād bhavati parjanyo yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ#All living beings subsist on food, and food is produced by rains. Rains come from the performance of sacrifice, and sacrifice is produced by the performance of prescribed duties.#annāt—from food

BG 3.15#कर्म ब्रह्मोद्भवं विद्धि ब्रह्माक्षरसमुद्भवम्\nतस्मात्सर्वगतं ब्रह्म नित्यं यज्ञे प्रतिष्ठितम्#karma brahmodbhavaṁ viddhi brahmākṣhara-samudbhavam\ntasmāt sarva-gataṁ brahma nityaṁ yajñe pratiṣhṭhitam#The duties for human beings are described in the Vedas, and the Vedas are manifested by God Himself. Therefore, the all-pervading Lord is eternally present in acts of sacrifice.#karma—duties

BG 3.16#एवं प्रवर्तितं चक्रं नानुवर्तयतीह य:\nअघायुरिन्द्रियारामो मोघं पार्थ स जीवति#evaṁ pravartitaṁ chakraṁ nānuvartayatīha yaḥ\naghāyur indriyārāmo moghaṁ pārtha sa jīvati#O Parth, those who do not accept their responsibility in the cycle of sacrifice established by the Vedas are sinful. They live only for the delight of their senses
column2: indeed their lives are in vain.#evam—thus
column10: indriya-ārāmaḥ—for the delight of their senses

column1: BG 3.17#यस्त्वात्मरतिरेव स्यादात्मतृप्तश्च मानव:\nआत्मन्येव च सन्तुष्टस्तस्य कार्यं न विद्यते#yas tvātma-ratir eva syād ātma-tṛiptaśh cha mānavaḥ\nātmanyeva cha santuṣhṭas tasya kāryaṁ na vidyate#But those who rejoice in the self, who are illumined and fully satisfied in the self, for them, there is no duty.#yaḥ—who

BG 3.18#नैव तस्य कृतेनार्थो नाकृतेनेह कश्चन\nन चास्य सर्वभूतेषु कश्चिदर्थव्यपाश्रय:#naiva tasya kṛitenārtho nākṛiteneha kaśhchana\nna chāsya sarva-bhūteṣhu kaśhchid artha-vyapāśhrayaḥ#Such self-realized souls have nothing to gain or lose either in discharging or renouncing their duties. Nor do they need to depend on other living beings to fulfill their self-interest.#na—not

BG 3.19#तस्मादसक्त: सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर\nअसक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुष:#tasmād asaktaḥ satataṁ kāryaṁ karma samāchara\nasakto hyācharan karma param āpnoti pūruṣhaḥ#Therefore, giving up attachment, perform actions as a matter of duty because by working without being attached to the fruits, one attains the Supreme.#tasmāt—therefore

BG 3.20#कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादय:
लोकसंग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जन:
स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते#karmaṇaiva hi sansiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ
loka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśhyan kartum arhasi
yad yad ācharati śhreṣhṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ
sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate#BG 3.20-21:#karmaṇā—by the performance of prescribed duties
column7: loka-saṅgraham—for the welfare of the masses

column1:
BG 3.21#कर्मणैव हि संसिद्धिमास्थिता जनकादय:
लोकसंग्रहमेवापि सम्पश्यन्कर्तुमर्हसि
यद्यदाचरति श्रेष्ठस्तत्तदेवेतरो जन:
स यत्प्रमाणं कुरुते लोकस्तदनुवर्तते#karmaṇaiva hi sansiddhim āsthitā janakādayaḥ
loka-saṅgraham evāpi sampaśhyan kartum arhasi
yad yad ācharati śhreṣhṭhas tat tad evetaro janaḥ
sa yat pramāṇaṁ kurute lokas tad anuvartate#BG 3.20-21:#karmaṇā—by the performance of prescribed duties
column7: loka-saṅgraham—for the welfare of the masses

column1: BG 3.22#न मे पार्थास्ति कर्तव्यं त्रिषु लोकेषु किञ्चन\nनानवाप्तमवाप्तव्यं वर्त एव च कर्मणि#na me pārthāsti kartavyaṁ triṣhu lokeṣhu kiñchana\nnānavāptam avāptavyaṁ varta eva cha karmaṇi#There is no duty for Me to do in all the three worlds, O Parth, nor do I have anything to gain or attain. Yet, I am engaged in prescribed duties.#na—not

BG 3.23#यदि ह्यहं न वर्तेयं जातु कर्मण्यतन्द्रित:\nमम वर्त्मानुवर्तन्ते मनुष्या: पार्थ सर्वश:#yadi hyahaṁ na varteyaṁ jātu karmaṇyatandritaḥ\nmama vartmānuvartante manuṣhyāḥ pārtha sarvaśhaḥ#For if I did not carefully perform the prescribed duties, O Parth, all men would follow My path in all respects.#yadi—if
column7: karmaṇi—in the performance of prescribed duties

column1: BG 3.24#उत्सीदेयुरिमे लोका न कुर्यां कर्म चेदहम्\nसङ्करस्य च कर्ता स्यामुपहन्यामिमा: प्रजा:#utsīdeyur ime lokā na kuryāṁ karma ched aham\nsankarasya cha kartā syām upahanyām imāḥ prajāḥ#If I ceased to perform prescribed actions, all these worlds would perish. I would be responsible for the pandemonium that would prevail, and would thereby destroy the peace of the human race.#utsīdeyuḥ—would perish

BG 3.25#सक्ता: कर्मण्यविद्वांसो यथा कुर्वन्ति भारत\nकुर्याद्विद्वांस्तथासक्तश्चिकीर्षुर्लोकसंग्रहम्#saktāḥ karmaṇyavidvānso yathā kurvanti bhārata\nkuryād vidvāns tathāsaktaśh chikīrṣhur loka-saṅgraham#As ignorant people perform their duties with attachment to the results, O scion of Bharat, so should the wise act without attachment, for the sake of leading people on the right path.#saktāḥ—attached

BG 3.26#न बुद्धिभेदं जनयेदज्ञानां कर्मसङ्गिनाम्\nजोषयेत्सर्वकर्माणि विद्वान्युक्त: समाचरन्#na buddhi-bhedaṁ janayed ajñānāṁ karma-saṅginām\njoṣhayet sarva-karmāṇi vidvān yuktaḥ samācharan#The wise should not create discord in the intellects of ignorant people, who are attached to fruitive actions, by inducing them to stop work. Rather, by performing their duties in an enlightened manner, they should inspire the ignorant also to do their prescribed duties.#na—not
column5: karma-saṅginām—who are attached to fruitive actions

column1: BG 3.27#प्रकृते: क्रियमाणानि गुणै: कर्माणि सर्वश:\nअहङ्कारविमूढात्मा कर्ताहमिति मन्यते#prakṛiteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśhaḥ\nahankāra-vimūḍhātmā kartāham iti manyate#All activities are carried out by the three modes of material nature. But in ignorance, the soul, deluded by false identification with the body, thinks of itself as the doer.#prakṛiteḥ—of material nature
column6: ahankāra-vimūḍha-ātmā—those who are bewildered by the ego and misidentify themselves with the body

column1: BG 3.28#तत्त्ववित्तु महाबाहो गुणकर्मविभागयो:\nगुणा गुणेषु वर्तन्त इति मत्वा न सज्जते#tattva-vit tu mahā-bāho guṇa-karma-vibhāgayoḥ\nguṇā guṇeṣhu vartanta iti matvā na sajjate#O mighty-armed Arjun, illumined persons distinguish the soul as distinct from guṇas and karmas. They perceive that it is only the guṇas (in the shape of the senses, mind, and others) that move among the guṇas (in the shape of the objects of perception), and thus they do not get entangled in them.#tattva-vit—knower of the truth
column6: guṇāḥ—modes of material nature in the shape of the senses, mind, and others
column7: guṇeṣhu—modes of material nature in the shape of objects of perception

column1: BG 3.29#प्रकृतेर्गुणसम्मूढा: सज्जन्ते गुणकर्मसु\nतानकृत्स्नविदो मन्दान्कृत्स्नविन्न विचालयेत्#prakṛiter guṇa-sammūḍhāḥ sajjante guṇa-karmasu\ntān akṛitsna-vido mandān kṛitsna-vin na vichālayet#Those who are deluded by the operation of the guṇas become attached to the results of their actions. But the wise who understand these truths should not unsettle such ignorant people who know very little.#prakṛiteḥ—of material nature
column2: guṇa—by the modes of material nature

column1: BG 3.30#मयि सर्वाणि कर्माणि संन्यस्याध्यात्मचेतसा\nनिराशीर्निर्ममो भूत्वा युध्यस्व विगतज्वर:#mayi sarvāṇi karmāṇi sannyasyādhyātma-chetasā\nnirāśhīr nirmamo bhūtvā yudhyasva vigata-jvaraḥ#Performing all works as an offering unto Me, constantly meditate on Me as the Supreme. Become free from desire and selfishness, and with your mental grief departed, fight!#mayi—unto me
column5: adhyātma-chetasā—with the thoughts resting on God
column6: nirāśhīḥ—free from hankering for the results of the actions

column1: BG 3.31#ये मे मतमिदं नित्यमनुतिष्ठन्ति मानवा:\nश्रद्धावन्तोऽनसूयन्तो मुच्यन्ते तेऽपि कर्मभि:#ye me matam idaṁ nityam anutiṣhṭhanti mānavāḥ\nśhraddhāvanto ’nasūyanto muchyante te ’pi karmabhiḥ#Those who abide by these teachings of Mine, with profound faith and free from envy, are released from the bondage of karma.#ye—who

BG 3.32#ये त्वेतदभ्यसूयन्तो नानुतिष्ठन्ति मे मतम्\nसर्वज्ञानविमूढांस्तान्विद्धि नष्टानचेतस:#ye tvetad abhyasūyanto nānutiṣhṭhanti me matam\nsarva-jñāna-vimūḍhāns tān viddhi naṣhṭān achetasaḥ#But those who find faults with My teachings, being bereft of knowledge and devoid of discrimination, they disregard these principles and bring about their own ruin.#ye—those

BG 3.33#सदृशं चेष्टते स्वस्या: प्रकृतेर्ज्ञानवानपि\nप्रकृतिं यान्ति भूतानि निग्रह: किं करिष्यति#sadṛiśhaṁ cheṣhṭate svasyāḥ prakṛiter jñānavān api\nprakṛitiṁ yānti bhūtāni nigrahaḥ kiṁ kariṣhyati#Even wise people act according to their natures, for all living beings are propelled by their natural tendencies. What will one gain by repression?#sadṛiśham—accordingly

BG 3.34#इन्द्रियस्येन्द्रियस्यार्थे रागद्वेषौ व्यवस्थितौ\nतयोर्न वशमागच्छेत्तौ ह्यस्य परिपन्थिनौ#indriyasyendriyasyārthe rāga-dveṣhau vyavasthitau\ntayor na vaśham āgachchhet tau hyasya paripanthinau#The senses naturally experience attachment and aversion to the sense objects, but do not be controlled by them, for they are way-layers and foes.#indriyasya—of the senses

BG 3.35#श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुण: परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात्\nस्वधर्मे निधनं श्रेय: परधर्मो भयावह:#śhreyān swa-dharmo viguṇaḥ para-dharmāt sv-anuṣhṭhitāt\nswa-dharme nidhanaṁ śhreyaḥ para-dharmo bhayāvahaḥ#It is far better to perform one’s natural prescribed duty, though tinged with faults, than to perform another’s prescribed duty, though perfectly. In fact, it is preferable to die in the discharge of one’s duty, than to follow the path of another, which is fraught with danger.#śhreyān—better

BG 3.36#अर्जुन उवाच
अथ केन प्रयुक्तोऽयं पापं चरति पूरुष:
अनिच्छन्नपि वार्ष्णेय बलादिव नियोजित:#arjuna uvācha
atha kena prayukto ’yaṁ pāpaṁ charati pūruṣhaḥ
anichchhann api vārṣhṇeya balād iva niyojitaḥ#Arjun asked: Why is a person impelled to commit sinful acts, even unwillingly, as if by force, O descendent of Vrishni (Krishna)?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said
column11: vārṣhṇeya—he who belongs to the Vrishni clan, Shree Krishna

column1:
BG 3.37#श्रीभगवानुवाच
काम एष क्रोध एष रजोगुणसमुद्भव: |
महाशनो महापाप्मा विद्ध्येनमिह वैरिणम्#śhrī bhagavān uvācha
kāma eṣha krodha eṣha rajo-guṇa-samudbhavaḥ
mahāśhano mahā-pāpmā viddhyenam iha vairiṇam#The Supreme Lord said: It is lust alone, which is born of contact with the mode of passion, and later transformed into anger. Know this as the sinful, all-devouring enemy in the world.#śhri-bhagavān uvācha—the Supreme Lord said

BG 3.38#धूमेनाव्रियते वह्निर्यथादर्शो मलेन च\nयथोल्बेनावृतो गर्भस्तथा तेनेदमावृतम्#dhūmenāvriyate vahnir yathādarśho malena cha\nyatholbenāvṛito garbhas tathā tenedam āvṛitam#Just as a fire is covered by smoke, a mirror is masked by dust, and an embryo is concealed by the womb, similarly one’s knowledge gets shrouded by desire.#dhūmena—by smoke

BG 3.39#आवृतं ज्ञानमेतेन ज्ञानिनो नित्यवैरिणा\nकामरूपेण कौन्तेय दुष्पूरेणानलेन च#āvṛitaṁ jñānam etena jñānino nitya-vairiṇā\nkāma-rūpeṇa kaunteya duṣhpūreṇānalena cha#The knowledge of even the most discerning gets covered by this perpetual enemy in the form of insatiable desire, which is never satisfied and burns like fire, O son of Kunti.#āvṛitam—covered

BG 3.40#इन्द्रियाणि मनो बुद्धिरस्याधिष्ठानमुच्यते\nएतैर्विमोहयत्येष ज्ञानमावृत्य देहिनम्#indriyāṇi mano buddhir asyādhiṣhṭhānam uchyate\netair vimohayatyeṣha jñānam āvṛitya dehinam#The senses, mind, and intellect are said to be breeding grounds of desire. Through them, it clouds one’s knowledge and deludes the embodied soul.#indriyāṇi—the senses

BG 3.41#तस्मात्त्वमिन्द्रियाण्यादौ नियम्य भरतर्षभ\nपाप्मानं प्रजहि ह्येनं ज्ञानविज्ञाननाशनम्#tasmāt tvam indriyāṇyādau niyamya bharatarṣhabha\npāpmānaṁ prajahi hyenaṁ jñāna-vijñāna-nāśhanam#Therefore, O best of the Bharatas, in the very beginning bring the senses under control and slay this enemy called desire, which is the embodiment of sin and destroys knowledge and realization.#tasmāt—therefore
column6: bharata-ṛiṣhabha—Arjun, the best of the Bharatas

column1: BG 3.42#इन्द्रियाणि पराण्याहुरिन्द्रियेभ्य: परं मन:\nमनसस्तु परा बुद्धिर्यो बुद्धे: परतस्तु स:#indriyāṇi parāṇyāhur indriyebhyaḥ paraṁ manaḥ\nmanasas tu parā buddhir yo buddheḥ paratas tu saḥ#The senses are superior to the gross body, and superior to the senses is the mind. Beyond the mind is the intellect, and even beyond the intellect is the soul.#indriyāṇi—senses

BG 3.43#एवं बुद्धे: परं बुद्ध्वा संस्तभ्यात्मानमात्मना\nजहि शत्रुं महाबाहो कामरूपं दुरासदम्#evaṁ buddheḥ paraṁ buddhvā sanstabhyātmānam ātmanā\njahi śhatruṁ mahā-bāho kāma-rūpaṁ durāsadam#Thus knowing the soul to be superior to the material intellect, O mighty armed Arjun, subdue the lower self (senses, mind, and intellect) by the higher self (strength of the soul), and kill this formidable enemy called lust.#evam—thus
column6: ātmānam—the lower self (senses, mind, and intellect)
